page: Amazon Web Services i4g.xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of average and weak performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests score in the average range at 1520.08 and 6056.47, respectively. However, memory bandwidth for small blocks is weak at 236.01 GB/sec, while large block read and write bandwidths are average at 71.67 GB/sec and 34.86 GB/sec. General computational benchmarks, such as Geekbench single-core at 969.0 and multi-core at 3224.0, fall into the weak category. Cryptographic and specialized instruction performance is poor, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 541.67 and an overall PassMark CPU score of 2687.46.

The i4g.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency by bundling a substantial 937 GB local NVMe SSD directly with ARM-based compute resources. This configuration reduces the need for external block storage attachments for data-intensive workloads. However, the tradeoff lies in its processing limitations; the instance exhibits weak performance in data compression, software compilation, and database operations, as well as poor performance in LLM inference tasks.

This instance is designed for workloads requiring local, low-latency storage combined with moderate memory capacity. Suitable applications include local database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose storage-heavy tasks that do not demand high-performance CPU instruction sets or intensive cryptographic processing.
description: Amazon Web Services i4g.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, 937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks indicate average results for stress-ng CPU tests, but weak outcomes for memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations. The instance presents a qualitative cost advantage by bundling high-capacity local NVMe storage with ARM-based compute, though it exhibits performance tradeoffs in cryptographic and compression tasks. The i4g.xlarge is suited for storage-heavy workloads, local database hosting, and caching applications that require high local storage density rather than intensive computational throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4g.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for low-latency database and storage workloads.

tagline: An ARM-powered, storage-optimized instance bundling dedicated physical cores with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
937 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Appropriate for local database hosting, caching, and storage-heavy workloads
